title: Preserve Structure across Lists and Link States
summary: Preserve Structure across Lists and Link States retrieves prior skills in an original survey checklist and transcript-navigation design review scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css list styling, css link states in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real survey checklist and transcript-navigation design review artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

steps.title: 04 · Read the survey checklist and transcript-navigation design review contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice survives changed data and devices? Scenario: survey checklist and transcript-navigation design review.
steps.why: Preserve Structure across Lists and Link States makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in survey checklist and transcript-navigation design review.

content.text: List markers, position, spacing, and ::marker presentation can improve scanning while the ul, ol, and li structure preserves grouping and sequence. Removing markers can reduce visible affordance and may affect semantics in some browser and assistive-technology combinations, especially when list-like display is also removed. Test the structure instead of treating bullets as decoration alone. Links must remain identifiable without color alone and expose meaningful link, visited, hover, focus-visible, and active states. LVHFA ordering can prevent broad state rules from unintentionally masking later interaction feedback. Hover cannot substitute for focus, visited styling has privacy restrictions, and removing underlines requires another durable non-color cue. Apply it to survey checklist and transcript-navigation design review.
